title: SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM - 211info
text: IMPACT NW BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER SUN YOUTH ADVOCACY PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides school-linked, age-appropriate, and culturally specific academic support that includes activities such as tutoring, mentoring, reading club, gender groups, and conflict resolution classes; case management, family engagement, and skill-building groups. 7211 SE 62nd Avenue Portland, OR 97206 Eligibility: Youth ages 12-17 who are at risk of academic failure Hours: Varies by service Email (503) 867-2188 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 867-2188 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Portland Public Schools SITE HOURS: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Services: Dropout Programs Parent/Family Involvement in Education Tutoring Services Youth Enrichment Programs Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY FACILITIES/CENTERS - BRENTWOOD-DARLINGTON COMMUNITY CENTER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HEALTHY FAMILIES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - INDEPENDENT LIVING PROGRAM INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT CHILD DEVELOPMENT SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - PARENT CHILD THERAPEUTIC SERVICES INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SAFE AND TOGETHER INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - SUN COMMUNITY SCHOOLS SOCIAL DEVELOPMENT AND ENRICHMENT - EARLY CHILDHOOD FATHERING PROJECT If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
